what_it_does: This file is a design breakdown noticeboard item that specifies a single, switchable annotation mechanism supporting a CONTENT mode (annotate document content) and a UI mode (annotate on-screen UI elements), and lists the implications and future extensions of that approach.
observations: Specifies two annotation modes: CONTENT and UI.
Both modes share one underlying selection-to-reply pipeline.
A 'commentable thing' is an addressable node, either a doc block (board://) or a UI element (ui://).
Mode is a lens that swaps target-source and some envelope fields, not a separate system.
Implies a registry of annotatable surfaces.
Implies a visible on-screen indicator of current mode/target.
UI mode requires the running app to expose UI element addresses to the annotation layer (inspector mode).
Future extensions include image, generated output, data row, and decision modes, plus mixed batches.
Frames the mechanism as a reusable company component that any surface can adopt.
standouts: Treats document blocks and live UI elements as equal addressable nodes in one pipeline.
Mode is a switchable lens rather than a separate implementation.
Ambition to make the mechanism a reusable company-wide annotation substrate.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history.
Body follows a SPECIFIED / IMPLIED / ADDITIONS breakdown structure.
Uses board:// URI style for internal item addresses and links via kind: part_of.
concerns: Does not define the envelope schema or which fields change per mode.
UI mode depends on runtime element address exposure that is not detailed here.
Future modes and mixed batches are stated as additions without constraints or priority.
The 'small registry of annotatable surfaces' is mentioned but not scoped.
notes: A breakdown arguing for one shared annotation pipeline across content and UI surfaces, with mode as a switchable lens rather than separate systems.
questions: What is the exact envelope schema and which fields differ between CONTENT and UI modes?
How are ui:// addresses generated, assigned, and kept stable across app versions?
What does the inspector mode look like and who triggers it?
Who maintains the registry of annotatable surfaces and how is a surface registered?
How are permissions, routing, and notifications handled differently per mode, if at all?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to break down 'two modes, one mechanism (content + UI),' and the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to unify feedback and annotation across documents and live applications, avoiding parallel comment systems by abstracting any annotatable thing as an addressable node.
novelty: Extending the same annotation pipeline to live UI elements (ui://) as first-class targets alongside document content (board://), rather than building a separate UI feedback tool.
contribution: The idea of a mode-agnostic, addressable-node annotation pipeline; the breakdown structure (SPECIFIED / IMPLIED / ADDITIONS); and the framing of the mechanism as a reusable company component that any surface can adopt.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard breakdown item specifying a unified, switchable annotation mechanism for both document content (board:// nodes) and on-screen UI elements (ui:// nodes), with implications and future extensions.
intention_for_embedding: To design a single target-agnostic feedback and annotation pipeline that can be reused across content and UI surfaces.
